team_context.cap_situation: tight — ~$35M in space after Goff restructure, but massive extensions due for Gibbs, Campbell, LaPorta, and Branch from the 2023 draft class. Cap gymnastics via restructures of Goff, St. Brown, Sewell, McNeill are keeping the window open but pushing dead money into future years.
team_context.trade_posture: Buyers at EDGE and OL, willing to listen on select secondary pieces. Team went 9-8 in 2025 and missed playoffs but believes core is intact. Lost Taylor Decker (released), David Montgomery (traded), Alex Anzalone (FA), Amik Robertson (FA). Need to balance competing now with extending homegrown stars.

players.name: Jared Goff
players.performance_evidence: 4,564 passing yards in 2025, 4x Pro Bowler, finished 5th in MVP voting in 2024. 117 receptions to ARSB alone. Three straight strong seasons. Led Lions to 15-2 in 2024 and NFC Championship in 2023. Slight step back in 2025 (9-8) but offense was 4th in scoring at 28.3 ppg.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise QB playing at a Pro Bowl level with 3 years of control. However, $37.6M cap hit in 2026 (rising to $62.6M and $69.6M) and ~$90M in dead cap make him functionally untradeable. Contract includes a no-trade clause. Value reflects talent but is academic — no trade is possible.

players.availability_reasoning: No-trade clause in contract. Dead cap of ~$90M makes any trade impossible. Franchise QB committed through 2028. Just restructured contract to clear $32M in space — team is building around him.

players.name: Teddy Bridgewater
players.performance_evidence: Completed 8 of 15 passes for 62 yards with Tampa in 2025. Previously backed up Goff in Detroit in 2023. Veteran presence, not a viable starter anymore. Signed on a cheap 1-year deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran backup QB with minimal on-field value. 33 years old on a cheap deal. Only worth a conditional late-round pick at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap veteran backup. Would listen to offers but minimal trade market. Signed specifically to mentor and back up Goff.

players.name: Jahmyr Gibbs
players.performance_evidence: 3x Pro Bowler. 1,839 scrimmage yards and 18 TDs in 2025. Career 5.5 YPC. Most touchdowns scored by any player in their first three seasons in NFL history. Dual-threat weapon: 1,223 rush yards + 616 receiving yards in 2025. PFF top-5 RB.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite young RB on a massive bargain rookie deal entering final year. Extension-eligible and projected for $60M+. Despite RB position devaluation, Gibbs' receiving ability and age (24) make him one of the most valuable RBs in football. Worth a late 1st to a contender, but Detroit will never trade him.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise cornerstone. Rapoport reported he's 'priority number one to be highly paid this offseason.' Team has publicly committed to extending him. Trading the best young RB in football would be franchise malpractice.

players.name: Isiah Pacheco
players.performance_evidence: 2x Super Bowl champion. Career 2,537 rushing yards and 14 TDs with Chiefs. Has declined in recent years — back-to-back seasons under 500 rushing yards averaging under 4.0 YPC. Signed to a cheap 1-year, $1.81M deal as Montgomery replacement.

trade_value.reasoning: Complementary back on a minimum deal. Declining production and injury concerns (broken leg in 2024) limit value. Worth a conditional 7th at best. Detroit signed him as a cheap depth piece behind Gibbs.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap replacement-level signing. If a team offered a late-round pick, Detroit would consider it, but there's little market for a declining RB on a minimum deal.
players.injury_notes: Fractured fibula in 2024 that limited his subsequent production. Two straight seasons under 500 rushing yards.

players.name: Amon-Ra St. Brown
players.performance_evidence: 117 receptions, 1,401 yards, 11 TDs in 2025. 4x consecutive Pro Bowl. 2x First-Team All-Pro (2023, 2024). PFF 90.7 overall grade (3rd among WRs) in 2025. 4 consecutive 100+ catch seasons. Top-5 WR in football.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old elite WR with 3 years of control on a contract that's becoming team-friendly as the market rises. Top-5 at his position, entering prime. However, $44.2M dead cap makes a trade functionally impossible. If it were possible, he'd command multiple 1sts.

players.availability_reasoning: Face of the franchise offense. $44.2M dead cap makes any trade impossible regardless. Just turned 26, playing the best football of his career. Zero chance Detroit moves him.
players.injury_notes: Dealt with knee and ankle injuries late in 2025 but played through them. Has played all 17 games in back-to-back seasons.

players.name: Jameson Williams
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2024: 58 catches, 1,001 yards, 7 TDs. 17.3 YPC (6th in NFL). Then signed 3-year, $80M extension. Continued development in 2025 with ~1,100 yards. Game-breaking speed, ascending talent. Led league in yards per catch among qualified WRs in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old ascending WR2 on a team-friendly cap hit ($8.2M in 2026) with 4 years of control. Breakout production and elite speed. Dead cap of $25.5M makes a trade very difficult. If tradeable, he'd command a 1st-round pick easily.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year, $80M extension. Team views him as a long-term core piece alongside St. Brown. Dead cap of $25.5M makes trade impossible. Only 24 and ascending.
players.injury_notes: ACL tear at Alabama delayed start of career. Fully healthy since.
players.character_notes: History of suspensions: 4-game gambling suspension in 2023, 2-game PED suspension in 2024. Multiple fines. Has cleaned up behavior since. Some risk baked into long-term deal.

players.name: Isaac TeSlaa
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who emerged as WR3 during 2025 season. Caught meaningful snaps and Dan Campbell has praised his trajectory. Still developing but carved out a role in a high-powered offense.

trade_value.reasoning: UDFA on a cheap deal with some upside but very limited track record. Worth a conditional late-round pick at most. More valuable to Detroit as cheap depth than as a trade chip.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap rotational piece. If a team offered a 6th or 7th round pick, Detroit would consider it. Not a core piece but useful depth.

players.name: Sam LaPorta
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl and 2nd-team All-Pro as a rookie (86 catches, 889 yards, 10 TDs in 2023). Production dipped in Year 2 (60/726/7) and Year 3 (40/489/3 in 9 games due to back injury). Still one of the best young TEs in football when healthy. Projected market value ~$17M/year.

trade_value.reasoning: Young Pro Bowl TE on a cheap final-year rookie deal. Back injury in 2025 adds risk. Extension-eligible and projected for $68M+ deal. If Detroit can't afford to extend all four 2023 picks, LaPorta is the likeliest trade candidate. Worth a mid-to-late 1st or early 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: ESPN's Graziano and Solak listed LaPorta as a potential trade candidate because Detroit has four 2023 draft picks to extend and may not be able to afford all of them. Back injury adds uncertainty. Lions still see themselves as contenders, so they'd need a clear overpay. But he has a price.
players.injury_notes: Season-ending back surgery in 2025 after missing 8 games. Only played 9 games. This is the key concern for any extension or trade.
players.trade_buzz: ESPN's Ben Solak and Dan Graziano listed him as a trade candidate in February 2026. League sources wonder if Lions can extend all four 2023 picks.

players.name: Brock Wright
players.performance_evidence: Primarily a blocking TE who fills in as a receiver when needed. Took on a larger role when LaPorta was injured. Solid but unspectacular. Good run blocker for Detroit's ground game.

trade_value.reasoning: Blocking TE with limited receiving upside on a cheap deal. Essentially replacement-level in a trade market. Worth a conditional 7th round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece. Would not block any trade for the right offer but minimal market exists.

players.name: Larry Borom
players.performance_evidence: Started 38 of 63 career games across Bears and Dolphins. Signed as a free agent to replace Taylor Decker. Better suited as a backup — 91.2% pass block win rate (32nd among qualified tackles). Serviceable but likely a placeholder until Detroit drafts a LT.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level starting tackle on a cheap deal. 26 years old with starting experience but limited upside. Worth a conditional late-round pick. Detroit likely views him as a bridge until they draft a LT.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed as a stopgap after Decker's release. If Detroit drafts a LT early, Borom becomes expendable depth. Cheap deal means easy to move.

players.name: Christian Mahogany
players.performance_evidence: Started as LG in 2025 after OL turnover. PFF pass-blocking grade of 40.7 — struggled significantly in pass protection. Still developing. 6th-round pick thrust into a starting role earlier than expected.

trade_value.reasoning: Young developmental guard on a minimum deal. Poor 2025 PFF grades limit trade value. More valuable to Detroit as cheap depth than on the open market. Worth a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap young player still developing. Detroit wouldn't shop him but would listen if someone offered a pick upgrade. Not a core piece.

players.name: Cade Mays
players.performance_evidence: Signed in free agency from Carolina on a 3-year, $25M deal with $14M guaranteed. Addressed Detroit's biggest need at center after Frank Ragnow's retirement and Graham Glasgow's release. Solid starter, not elite.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting center on a fair deal just signed in free agency. 3 years of control. Dead cap of $9M limits trade flexibility in Year 1. Worth a 3rd-round pick but Detroit just signed him to start — not moving him.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to fill a critical need. Would take a significant overpay to move him since they'd have to find another center. Not being shopped.

players.name: Tate Ratledge
players.performance_evidence: Started all 17 games at RG as a rookie in 2025. PFF overall grade of 70.3 — inconsistent but showed flashes. 24 pressures and 2 sacks allowed in 620 pass-blocking snaps. First-Team All-American at Georgia in 2024. High ceiling, developing.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 2nd-round pick on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. All-American pedigree at Georgia but inconsistent rookie year. High upside makes him valuable to Detroit. Worth a 3rd-round pick but Lions have no reason to trade him.

players.availability_reasoning: Day 2 pick entering Year 2 with high upside. Detroit invested the 57th overall pick in him and he started every game. Would need a massive overpay to move. Not being shopped.

players.name: Penei Sewell
players.performance_evidence: 2x First-Team All-Pro (2023, 2024). 2x Pro Bowl. PFF named him their best OL of the 2023 season (93.1 run-blocking grade). Allowed just 3 sacks over 2 seasons. Anchor of Detroit's elite OL. Contract already looking like a bargain as OT market rises past $28M/year.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old All-Pro tackle on a team-friendly deal with 4 years of control. Top-3 OT in football. Would command an absurd haul in any trade — but $48.9M dead cap makes it impossible. This is a franchise cornerstone.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise cornerstone. 25 years old, 2x All-Pro, on a deal that's becoming a bargain. $48.9M dead cap makes trade literally impossible. Dan Campbell called him 'our foundation.'
players.injury_notes: Remarkably durable — has started all but one game since being drafted.

players.name: Aidan Hutchinson
players.performance_evidence: 14.5 sacks in 2025 (4th in NFL), 54 total tackles, 4 forced fumbles. PFF 91.9 overall grade (4th among EDGE). Career 43.0 sacks in 4 seasons. Returned from catastrophic broken tibia/fibula in 2024 to put up All-Pro numbers. 4-year, $180M extension signed Oct 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old elite pass rusher on an extremely team-friendly cap hit ($10.1M in 2026, $16M in 2027) with 5 years of control. Comeback Player of the Year candidate. Top-5 EDGE in football. Dead cap of $51.9M makes trade impossible. Would command the farm if it were possible.

players.availability_reasoning: Homegrown star, face of the defense. Just signed $180M extension. Cap hit is a bargain for the next 3 years due to option bonus structure. $51.9M dead cap. Zero chance of trade under any circumstances.
players.injury_notes: Fractured tibia and fibula in October 2024. Made full recovery and played all 17 games in 2025 at an elite level. Medical concern has been fully alleviated.

players.name: Alim McNeill
players.performance_evidence: 5.0 sacks and 88.5 PFF grade in 2023. Signed 4-year, $97M extension in Oct 2024 ($24.25M AAV, 4th among DTs). However, tore ACL in Week 15 of 2024 and knee issues have caused him to miss 14 games over last 3 seasons. When healthy, he's a top-10 interior DL.

trade_value.reasoning: Talented DT on a big contract but ACL tear in 2024 and persistent knee issues are major concerns. Spotrac noted Lions may be 'weary of moving money around too much' on his deal. Post-June 1 trade would save $24M but finding a partner is unlikely given injury risk. Worth a 2nd if healthy, but injury risk drops value significantly.

players.availability_reasoning: ACL injury and persistent knee issues create uncertainty. Dead cap and contract structure make a trade difficult but not impossible post-June 1. Detroit would prefer to keep him if healthy but the contract could become problematic if knee doesn't fully recover.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL in Week 15 of 2024 (Bills game). Has missed 14 games over last 3 seasons due to knee issues. Spotrac flagged knee concerns regarding cap flexibility. This is a serious factor in his trade value.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters have noted the Lions' options with his contract are limited. Post-June 1 trade would provide meaningful cap savings but market for an injured DT on a $24M/year deal is slim.

players.name: Levi Onwuzurike
players.performance_evidence: Former 2nd-round pick who has battled injuries throughout career. Still under contract due to a rare CBA rule (tolled year). Depth interior DL. Pressures but has struggled to convert them into sacks.

trade_value.reasoning: Former Day 2 pick who never panned out. Rotational DT on a cheap deal. Minimal trade value — worth a conditional 7th at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Former high pick who hasn't lived up to billing. On a cheap deal with minimal dead cap. Detroit would happily move him for any draft pick compensation.
players.injury_notes: Has battled chronic back issues throughout career. Previously placed on IR.

players.name: Tyler Lacy
players.performance_evidence: Depth EDGE rusher. Detroit's pass rush beyond Hutchinson and Al-Quadin Muhammad (now a free agent) was minimal — non-Hutchinson/Muhammad EDGEs recorded just 2.5 sacks in 2025. EDGE is a massive team need.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth EDGE on a minimum deal. Essentially no trade value. Detroit needs to ADD pass rushing help, not subtract it.

players.availability_reasoning: Low-end depth piece. Detroit would listen but there's no market. Team actually needs to upgrade significantly at DE2.

players.name: Malcolm Rodriguez
players.performance_evidence: Fan favorite from Hard Knocks 2022. Played just 7 games in 2025, primarily special teams — only 70 defensive snaps. Coming off torn ACL from 2024. Re-signed on a 1-year prove-it deal. Had an 87.1 PFF run defense grade before the ACL in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth LB coming off a torn ACL on a prove-it deal. Minimal trade value. Detroit re-signed him specifically for depth and special teams.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap depth signing. Would listen but no meaningful trade market for a LB coming off an ACL on a minimum deal.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL in August 2024. Played only 7 games in 2025 with 70 defensive snaps. Recovery is the key question for 2026.

players.name: Jack Campbell
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025 season — team's leading tackler and defensive signal-caller. Former 1st-round pick from Iowa who has developed into the Lions' defensive quarterback. Extension-eligible; projected for $17M+/year deal. Fifth-year option projected at $22.99M.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old ascending LB on a cheap final-year rookie deal. Extension-eligible with a fifth-year option decision due by May 1. Off-ball LB position limits the ceiling, but his age, production, and cheap contract make him very valuable. Worth a late 1st/early 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: Core defensive piece that Detroit wants to extend. However, the franchise tag for LBs would cost $28M+, creating leverage. If Detroit can't afford to extend all four 2023 picks, Campbell's extension might come first (per Graziano). Would take a massive overpay to move him.
players.trade_buzz: Graziano reported Campbell's extension may be prioritized ahead of Gibbs due to franchise tag cost dynamics. Lions want to keep him.

players.name: Derrick Barnes
players.performance_evidence: 194 tackles, 12 TFL, 4 sacks over 4 seasons. Signed 3-year, $25.5M extension ($16M guaranteed) in March 2025. Game-sealing INT in 2023 NFC Divisional Round. Reliable starter who excels against the run.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid starter on a reasonable deal with 2 years of control. However, $19.7M dead cap in 2026 makes a trade essentially impossible this year. In 2027, dead cap drops to ~$2.1M, making him moveable then. For now, he's locked in.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed an extension. Fully guaranteed 2026 salary and $19.7M dead cap make a 2026 trade impossible. Essentially locked in for at least 2 years. Not being shopped.

players.name: D.J. Reed
players.performance_evidence: Signed 3-year, $48M deal ($32M guaranteed) in FA 2025 from Jets. Solid CB1 who dealt with hamstring issues in 2025. Reliable in coverage with experience in both man and zone schemes. Not elite but a solid starter at a premium position.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting CB with 2 years of control on a $16M/year deal. However, $26.7M dead cap makes a trade impossible in 2026. 2026 salary is fully guaranteed. Locked in this year.

players.availability_reasoning: Fully guaranteed 2026 salary and $26.7M dead cap make a 2026 trade impossible. Lions signed him as their CB1 and need him with secondary injuries. After 2026, there's a natural exit point with the option bonus decision in 2027.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury in 2025 that caused him to miss games. Hutchinson specifically mentioned getting Reed healthy as a priority for 2026.

players.name: Terrion Arnold
players.performance_evidence: First-round pick who had a rough 2024 rookie year with frequent penalties. Improved in press/man coverage as season progressed. Missed time in 2025 with a shoulder injury (Spotrac noted a 'Week 5 season-ending shoulder injury'). Still developing at a premium position with a 5th-year option for 2028.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old former 1st-round CB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control plus a 5th-year option. Shoulder injury and inconsistent play lower value from draft slot. Spotrac's Ginnitti noted he 'could be quietly shopped.' Cost-controlled at a premium position — worth an early 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac's Ginnitti flagged Arnold as a player who could be quietly shopped. Shoulder injury and disappointing early results create doubt. With 2 years + 5th-year option remaining at $4.85M guaranteed, other teams would be interested. Lions 'always listen' per beat reporters, but moving him now would be selling low.
players.injury_notes: Season-ending shoulder injury in Week 5 of 2025 season. Rough rookie year in 2024 with frequent penalties. Development is a question mark entering Year 3.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac's Michael Ginnitti noted Arnold 'could be quietly shopped in the coming months' due to underwhelming results and injury concerns. Beat reporters say this is more due diligence by other teams than Detroit actively shopping him.

players.name: Ennis Rakestraw Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Played just 8 games total — missed all of 2025 with a shoulder injury sustained in the first training camp practice. In limited action, allowed 12 catches for 69 scoreless yards on 18 targets. Allowed just 0.47 yards per coverage snap in the slot. Holmes called him his No. 2 CB on the board after Arnold.

trade_value.reasoning: 2nd-round pick with high upside but essentially a redshirt year due to injury. Very cheap deal with 3 years of control. Potential starting NB who hasn't proven it yet. Worth a 4th-round pick based on draft pedigree and upside, but Lions drafted him to be their future slot CB.

players.availability_reasoning: Detroit invested a 2nd-round pick and views him as their future slot corner, potentially replacing Amik Robertson. Would need a significant offer to part with him given they haven't seen his full potential yet.
players.injury_notes: Shoulder injury in first training camp practice of 2025 that cost him the entire season. Played just 8 career games. Health is the primary concern.

players.name: Brian Branch
players.performance_evidence: 258 total tackles, 20 TFLs, 14 QB hits, 38 pass deflections, 7 INTs over 43 career games. PFF's No.1-graded safety in 2024 with top-3 grades in coverage, run defense, and pass rushing. Versatile weapon who plays safety, slot, and blitzes. Was on pace for best season before Achilles tear in Dec 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: When healthy, Branch is arguably the best safety in football at just 24. On a dirt-cheap final-year rookie deal ($2.55M cap hit). However, Achilles tear in December 2025 creates significant uncertainty about 2026 availability (8-12 month recovery). Extension will be delayed until Lions can assess recovery. Pre-injury, he'd be untouchable. Post-Achilles, there's a sliver of availability if someone offers a massive haul.

players.availability_reasoning: Achilles tear in December 2025 means 8-12 month recovery — may miss significant time in 2026. Extension talks will be delayed. Spotrac projects $80M extension when healthy. Lions have publicly said they want to keep him, but the injury and the need to extend 3 other 2023 picks creates slim possibility of a trade if someone offers a 1st. Very unlikely but not zero.
players.injury_notes: Torn Achilles in Week 14 (December 2025) vs Cowboys. 8-12 month recovery window. May miss significant time in 2026. This is a major injury for a player who relies on explosiveness and change-of-direction.
players.character_notes: Some headlines for conduct after a primetime loss to Kansas City in 2025, but nothing that materially affects trade value.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac projects a 4-year, $80M extension. Lions will delay extension talks until they assess Achilles recovery. Multiple reporters note Lions may not be able to extend all four 2023 picks.

players.name: Kerby Joseph
players.performance_evidence: Led NFL with 9 INTs in 2024. First-Team All-Pro 2024 — first Lions safety to earn that since 1962. 17 career INTs lead all NFL players over last 3 seasons. Signed 4-year, $86M extension making him 2nd-highest-paid safety in NFL. However, chronic knee injury limited him to 6 games in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: When healthy, Joseph is an All-Pro ballhawk and one of football's best safeties. But chronic knee injury described as 'not necessarily going away' by Dan Campbell is alarming. $15M dead cap and option-bonus-heavy contract structure make a trade very painful. Cap hit is a bargain ($5.8M in 2026) but rises sharply. Worth a late 1st when healthy, but injury risk is severe.

players.availability_reasoning: The knee situation is deeply concerning — former NFL doctor David Chao was 'not optimistic' about Joseph's NFL future. Lions will give him every chance to get healthy for 2026, but if the knee doesn't improve, this contract ($86M with massive option bonuses) could become one of the worst in football. Complex contract structure with option bonuses means dead cap is actually much higher than sites display (~$15M+ if cut after 2026).
players.injury_notes: Chronic knee injury (cartilage wear, bone bruise) that is described as a condition that 'doesn't fix itself.' Missed 11 games in 2025. Joseph himself said his knee was 'messed up.' Former NFL doctor Chao was not optimistic. This is a potentially career-altering situation.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters have extensively discussed the financial implications of Joseph's knee. If he can't return to form, the Lions face a major contract problem. No active trade talk, but the uncertainty is real.
